name: MCP Server Management
description: Manage Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers conversationally — list, add, remove, and assign servers to agents without editing JSON files. Each user manages their own servers (stored in users/<id>/mcp.json). After any mutation the tool cache is refreshed so changes take effect immediately, no server restart needed.
intent_examples: what MCP servers do I have
assign the MCP server to my coder
give the MCP tools to Sydney

function.name: mcp_list_servers
function.description: List the user's currently registered MCP servers, including which agents each is assigned to. Returns id, displayName, transport, command, args, env keys (values redacted), and assignedToAgents for each.

function.name: mcp_add_server
function.description:
Register a new MCP server for this user. Supports two transports:

**stdio** — launches a subprocess on the OE host. Required: command + args.
  Example (GitHub):
    id: "github"
    transport: "stdio"
    command: "npx"
    args: ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
    env: { "GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN": "ghp_xxx" }

**http** — connects to a remote MCP server over HTTP/SSE. Required: url. Auth via headers (typically `Authorization: Bearer <token>`).
  Example (remote server):
    id: "my-remote"
    transport: "http"
    url: "https://mcp.example.com/sse"
    headers: { "Authorization": "Bearer xxx" }

After adding, the tool cache is refreshed automatically — assigned agents see the new tools on their next turn. If the server fails to connect, you'll get an error message; the entry is still persisted so you can fix the config later.

id.description: Short unique id for this server within the user's config. Letters, digits, hyphens only. NO double-underscores (reserved as namespace separator).

displayName.description: Friendly name shown to the user. Optional — defaults to the id.

transport.description: Transport: 'stdio' for local subprocesses or 'http' for remote MCP servers.

args.description: stdio only: args passed to the command.

env.description: stdio only: env vars passed to the subprocess (e.g. API keys). Sensitive values are encrypted at rest.

headers.description: http only: request headers, typically including Authorization for auth. Encrypted at rest.

assignedToAgents.description: Agent ids that should receive this server's tools.

function.name: mcp_remove_server
function.description: Remove an MCP server from this user's config. The server is unregistered and its tools disappear from all assigned agents on the next refresh (triggered automatically). Idempotent — removing an unknown id is a no-op.

function.name: mcp_assign_server
function.description: Add an agent to a server's assignedToAgents list (so the agent sees the server's tools). Idempotent. Refresh fires automatically.

function.name: mcp_unassign_server
function.description: Remove an agent from a server's assignedToAgents list. Idempotent. Refresh fires automatically.

function.name: mcp_refresh
function.description: Re-read this user's mcp.json from disk, reconnect to each server, and refresh the tool cache. Useful after manual edits to mcp.json or when a server was restarted out-of-band. The other mutation tools (add/remove/assign/unassign) refresh automatically — you don't need to call this after them.

systemPromptAddition:
## MCP server management — USE THESE TOOLS YOURSELF

Any question or command involving "MCP" / "MCP server" / "Model Context Protocol" — list, add, remove, assign, unassign, refresh — is handled by the `mcp_*` tools listed in your toolset (`mcp_list_servers`, `mcp_add_server`, `mcp_remove_server`, `mcp_assign_server`, `mcp_unassign_server`, `mcp_refresh`). **CALL THEM DIRECTLY. DO NOT DELEGATE to Ada, the coder, or any specialist.** These tools mutate `users/<id>/mcp.json` through proper writers; the coder editing the JSON file by hand would corrupt the format and bypass cache refresh.

Examples:
- "remove the everything MCP server" → call `mcp_remove_server({id:"everything"})` immediately.
- "list my MCP servers" → call `mcp_list_servers()` immediately.
- "add github" / "connect my notion" / "set up the filesystem MCP" — look up the official server's npm package via WebSearch / WebFetch if you don't already know it, then call `mcp_add_server` with the right command + args + env hints. For sensitive values (API tokens, OAuth secrets), ASK {{USER_NAME}} for the value explicitly — don't guess and don't read from the environment.

After adding a server, briefly confirm which agents now have its tools, and offer to demonstrate one of them so {{USER_NAME}} sees it work.
